violations.text: Raw animal foods (shelled eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (cheese) in the reach-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2025

Cheese and tomatoes was observed held at 56-58 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2025
Prep unit located in the kitchen area was not in good repair, ambient observed at 55F. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2025
Cooking utensils in the kitchen area are displayed with the food contact surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2025
Inspector observed single service items stored on the floor. Food and single service items must be stored 6 inches off the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the floor mixer shield have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and/or other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2025

Inspector observed food prep sink in the kitchen area lacking a back flow prevention. An approved backflow prevention device is required in order to prevent backflow of a solid, liquid, or gas contaminant into the water supply at each point of use at a food establishment. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025
